The '''''Admonitor''''' was an upgraded [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] under the [[Empire of the Hand]] and served as the [[Command ship/Legends|command vessel]], and personal [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]]. Thrawn had use of this [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] during the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] exploration and conquering the vast Unknown Regions for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. 

==History==
[[File:Admonitor.jpg|thumb|left|The ''Admonitor'' in space.]]
The ''Admonitor''{{'s}} first reported commander was [[Dagon Niriz]]. When [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] was assigned to the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], it was this ship which he departed on; his longtime associate, [[Voss Parck/Legends|Voss Parck]], was assigned as the ship's first officer.<ref name="CD" />

One of the ''Admonitor''{{'s}} first [[Skirmish between the Admonitor and Creysis's scout squadron|encounters]] on this mission was with [[Ebruchi]] [[Ebruchi|pirates]], which Thrawn was able to deal with effectively.<ref name="CD" />

Later, the ''Admonitor'' returned briefly to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] so Thrawn could assist [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] in the [[Capture of Zekka Thyne|removal]] of [[Zekka Thyne]], and thus [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun's]] influence, from [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]].<ref name="Side Trip" />

[[File:BattleofCarida-FoC.jpg|thumb|left|The ''Admonitor'' in the Space Battle at [[Carida/Legends|Carida]].]]

It is likely that it was during the course of these events that Thrawn participated in a [[Space Battle at Carida|space battle]] over [[Carida/Legends|Carida]] with [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Tyber Zann]]. The head of [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]], [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]], had arranged a meeting between Imperial contacts and Zann so he could sell a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] artifact to the Empire. When Zann's freighter arrived, it was met by the ''Admonitor'' leading an Imperial fleet, and Thrawn double-crossed the crimelord by informing him of Xizor's death and demanding the artifact be turned over in exchange for a quick and honorable death. Zann responded by having the rest of the fleet he had stationed nearby make a short [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jump into the system. In turn Thrawn fired a hidden [[HVs-2 hypervelocity gun|hypervelocity gun]] on the planet's surface, destroying an [[Interceptor IV-class frigate|Interceptor IV frigate]]. Finally, Zann brought his new flagship, the [[Merciless (Aggressor-class)|''Merciless'']], out of hyperspace, and the two fleets engaged. Soon after, Zann was again betrayed by his henchman [[Bossk/Legends|Bossk]], who stole the Sith Artifact and absconded with it to the ''Admonitor'' aboard his ship, the [[Hound's Tooth/Legends|''Hound's Tooth'']]. Thrawn, outnumbered but with his prize secured, retreated, leaving the rest of the fleet to its defeat. However, Zann, having predicted this betrayal, had his lieutenant [[Urai Fen]] attach a homing device to the artifact, which eventually led Zann to [[Imperial Archives (museum)/Legends|Palpatine's Museum]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="FoC" />

During his time in the Unknown Regions, Thrawn and the ''Admonitor'' waged a war with a mostly-primitive local species. He discovered that a military commander of that species had crafted three wooden statues with gem incrustations that had been acquired by [[Yarkora/Legends|Yarkora]] antiques dealer [[Saell-Terae]]. Thrawn, wanting to understand his enemy by studying their art, offered to buy the artworks from Saell-Terae, but the Yarkora refused. Saell-Terae eventually had to escape from Thrawn's forces and abandoned his ship, the ''[[Lost Reef]]'', with the statues still hidden inside.<ref name="ss">''[[Stock Ships]]''</ref>

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]]<!-- date can be sourced to Crisis of Faith --> after the [[Attack on the Red City|battle]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Quethold]] and the death of [[Nuso Esva]], the ''Admonitor'' left Quethold to cleanup Esva's legacy in the [[Chaos Triangle]] of the Unknown Regions.<ref name="Crisis of Faith">{{StoryCite|story=Crisis of Faith|book=Heir to the Empire: The 20th Anniversary Edition}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
In ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption]]'', the ''Admonitor'' is colored a pale blue, in contrast to the Imperial gray of all other Star Destroyers.
